title: carum alpinum subsp. macuca
intro: Carum alpinum subsp. macuca, also known as Himalayan Carrot or Wild Carrot, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the carrot family (Apiaceae). The medicinal properties of this plant include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which make it useful in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Carum alpinum subsp. macuca include reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and helping to manage respiratory problems such as bronchitis and asthma. The herbal preparations of this plant include essential oils, infusions, and dried roots, which can be used to make teas, tinctures, and other remedies.
